metric_value: 38 studies and 1,042 observations spanning the complete lifecycle of the Aberdeen-Maxtor midline storage engagement 2002-2004
notes: Verified by direct count of archived studies and observations in the repository

metric_value: Most complete surviving record of analyst-led category creation in enterprise IT
notes: Based on comparison with available public records of comparable engagements; no equivalent full-lifecycle archive identified

metric_value: Confirmed: SATA became dominant secondary storage medium by 2006-2008
notes: Cross-referenced against IDC and Gartner storage market data

metric_value: Confirmed: every major storage vendor launched ILM programs; tiered storage became standard architecture
notes: EMC NetApp HP IBM HDS all launched ILM initiatives 2004-2010

metric_value: Confirmed: EMC Data Domain and NetApp ONTAP automated tiering matured 2009-2011 matching the predicted 2008-2009 window
notes: Computerworld article prediction verified against product release dates

metric_value: Confirmed: by 2008-2010 disk-to-disk backup was enterprise standard with tape relegated to deep archive
notes: Industry transition well-documented in Gartner Magic Quadrant for backup

metric_value: Confirmed: HIPAA enforcement and SEC Rule 17a-4 drove fixed-content storage demand as predicted
notes: DiFranco 2003 prediction verified against regulatory implementation timeline

metric_name: Storage vendor revenue pressure prediction accuracy
metric_value: Confirmed: EMC HDS IBM experienced storage hardware revenue headwinds 2004-2006
notes: RAMP survey report prediction verified against vendor financial reports

metric_value: Hill's 2002 four-pool taxonomy (Online/Midline/Nearline/Offline) maps directly to AWS S3 storage classes (Standard/Intelligent-Tiering/Glacier/Deep Archive)
notes: Architectural correspondence identified by AI analysis; not an explicit prediction but a demonstrated intellectual lineage

metric_value: Face-to-face interviews with State Street Kaiser Permanente Visa/Inovant Applied Materials and Timex provide detailed enterprise storage snapshots with exact capacity utilization and growth figures
Survey finding that enterprise storage utilization ran at 60%+ directly contradicted vendor-promoted low-utilization narrative — genuine independent analysis not sponsored validation
notes: Key indicator of research independence and analytical integrity

metric_value: Pools of Storage Decision Tool (four-stage workflow: Solution + Competitive Maturity Grid + Financial Framework + Case for Action) anticipated SaaS assessment-tool category
notes: After-action valued tool R&D at $75K in reusable IP; Macromedia EVP Al Ramadan expressed interest

metric_value: Midline category was broadly adopted but Maxtor acquired by Seagate 2006 before achieving category leadership — Siebel/CRM analogy did not fully complete
notes: Maxtor acquisition price $1.9B; category survived sponsor

metric_value: $60K Phase 1 + $128K Phase 2 = approximately $188K total contracted work; 150 days actual vs 60 days planned; 16-person project team
notes: Figures from SOW and after-action review documents

metric_value: Project succeeded in defining the category and failed to install the sponsor as permanent leader — documents the boundary between market engineering and competitive execution
notes: Core strategic lesson of the engagement
